hero.subtitle: A zero-knowledge DSL with dual execution — run your code in a full VM or compile it to arithmetic circuits. Same syntax, two targets.

features.title: Why Achronyme
features.subtitle: Other ZK tools force you to choose between expressiveness and efficiency. Achronyme gives you both.

dual.title: Dual Execution
dual.desc: One language, two targets. Write closures, recursion, and GC-managed collections in VM mode. The same syntax compiles to R1CS or Plonkish circuits. No separate circuit language needed.

proofs.title: First-Class Proofs
proofs.desc: prove {} blocks return real proof objects. Chain them, extract components with proof_json(), verify with verify_proof(). Proofs are values, not CLI artifacts.

native.title: Native Provers
native.desc: Groth16 and PlonK run in-process — a single Rust binary, zero external dependencies. No Node.js, no snarkjs ceremony dance, no 5-step CLI workflow.

security.title: Compile-Time Safety
security.desc: Under-constrained circuits are catastrophic ZK bugs. Achronyme's taint analysis detects unproven witness variables before a single proof is generated.

snarkjs.title: On-Chain Verification
snarkjs.desc: Export .r1cs and .wtns files for snarkjs. Generate Solidity verifier contracts with --solidity and verify proofs directly on Ethereum.

open.title: Optimized Circuits
open.desc: Boolean propagation tracks proven-boolean variables and skips redundant constraints. Constant folding and dead code elimination shrink circuits automatically.

comparison.title: The difference
comparison.subtitle: Proving a Poseidon commitment: Circom + snarkjs vs Achronyme.
